rulings.Case Title: DANIEL E. OSUNDE VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS TRUSTEE FOR et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege in good faith causes of action for fraud/deceit and cancelation of instruments in light of arguments raised in demurrer. No opposition filed. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE MARIE ANNUZZI et al VS. HENRY FONG PAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT HENRY PAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as to the contract cause of action for Plaintiff to allege specific provisions of the lease that have been breached and how they have been breached. Demurrer to the cause of action for constructive eviction is overruled. "Conditions allowed to exist in a portion of the building other than the demised premises may cause a constructive eviction." Buckner v. Azulai (1967) 251 Cal. App. Supp. 2nd 1030; Butt v. Bertola (1952) 110 Cal.App.2d 128; Groh v. Kover's Bull Pen, Inc. (1963) 221 Cal.App.2d 611. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment is overruled. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts regarding actions of third-parties attributable to the landlord. Nativi v. Deutshce Bank (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 261; Restatement 2nd Property, paragraph 6.1, illustrations 10-12. Demurer to the causes of action for breach of warranty of habitability, good faith and fair dealing is overruled. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support these causes of action re: inadequate garbage receptacles and rent increase. First Amended Complaint is not uncertain. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE MARIE ANNUZZI et al VS. HENRY FONG PAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT HENRY PAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support punitive damages request based on conscious disregard of Plaintiff's rights and/or safety. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE MARGARET CHAVEZ VS. GEORGE TRAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANTs GEORGE TRAN and DWIGHT TRAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address all issues raised by demurrer. No opposition filed. Moving parties' counsel is ordered again (See CUD-14-647826 March 18, 2014 minute order) to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Failure to comply again may result in a motion being taken off calendar. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GARY YEUNG VS. MICHAEL KLOTSMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL KLOTSMAN's Motion To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For Attorney's Fees DENIED. The right to possession by the landlord did not have its inception in some sort of tortious conduct by the lessee. Neither "60 Day Notice" nor complaint allege a tort allegedly committed by defendant and triggering plaintiff's right to seek possession by first serving notice, and then filing the UD action. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. GEORGINA MUNOZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT GEORGINA MUNOZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to attach physically unaltered copy of the written agreement. RJN of Complaint in case CUD-13-646128 is granted. Moving party and its counsel are ordered again to comply with SFLR 2.6C (See CUD-14-649316 October 22, 2014 Minute Order; CUD-13-346270 October 11, 2013 Minute Order) to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Failure to comply again will result in a motion being taken off calendar. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN J COYNE VS. DIEGO DE LEO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Diego Deleo'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 17, 2014, line 6. Defendant Diego Deleo's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint GRANTED. Notice of Termination is fatally defective re: description of property to be withdrawn/tenancy to be terminated. = (501/RBU)
